title: Faith
question: Have I denied or abandoned the Catholic faith (apostasy)?
description: Apostasy is the total repudiation of the Christian faith (CCC 2089). It is grave matter because it rejects God’s revelation and severs communion with the Church.
counsels: Return through Confession and a profession of faith.
Study the core teachings you rejected.
Seek guidance from a priest or mentor.
prevention: Stay connected to a parish community

title: Faith
question: Have I seriously doubted or rejected a doctrine of the Church (heresy)?
description: Heresy is obstinate post-baptismal denial of a truth to be believed with divine faith (CCC 2089).
counsels: Clarify what you rejected and why.
Seek truth with humility, not pride.
Bring intellectual doubts to someone faithful.

title: Faith
question: Have I engaged in superstition, horoscopes, or occult practices?
description: All forms of divination and occult practices reject trust in God (CCC 2116–2117).
counsels: Renounce explicitly in prayer.
Remove all related objects/apps.
Confess and replace with trust in God.

title: Hope
question: Have I fallen into despair, believing God cannot forgive me?
description: Despair rejects God's mercy and salvation (CCC 2091).
counsels: God’s mercy is infinite.
Confess and trust Him again.

title: Hope
question: Have I presumed God's mercy without repentance?
description: Presumption expects forgiveness without conversion (CCC 2092).
counsels: Repent sincerely.
Avoid excusing sin.

title: Love of God
question: Have I missed Sunday Mass without serious reason?
description: Missing Mass deliberately is grave (CCC 2181).
counsels: Make Mass a priority.
Confess if missed deliberately.

title: Prayer Life
question: Have I neglected daily prayer?
description: Prayer is necessary for spiritual life (CCC 2744).
counsels: Start with 5–10 minutes daily.

title: Chastity
question: Have I engaged in fornication?
description: Sex outside marriage is gravely sinful (CCC 2353).
counsels: End sinful relationships.
Seek accountability.

title: Chastity
question: Have I viewed pornography?
description: Pornography violates dignity and purity (CCC 2354).
counsels: Confess immediately.
Install blockers.

title: Chastity
question: Have I committed masturbation?
description: Masturbation is disordered use of sexuality (CCC 2352).
counsels: Confess and seek discipline.

title: Chastity
question: Have I committed adultery?
description: Adultery violates the marriage covenant (CCC 2380).
counsels: End the relationship immediately.
Seek reconciliation.

title: Love of Neighbor
question: Have I been abusive toward my spouse or children?
description: Abuse violates dignity and charity (CCC 2302).
counsels: Seek help immediately.
Repent sincerely.

title: Justice
question: Have I failed to provide for my family?
description: Neglecting family responsibilities is unjust (CCC 2211).
counsels: Take responsibility seriously.

title: Obedience
question: Have I disobeyed legitimate authority?
description: Religious obedience imitates Christ (CCC 915).
counsels: Practice humility.

title: Chastity
question: Have I violated my vow of chastity?
description: Consecrated chastity is total dedication to God (CCC 2349).
counsels: Return to grace immediately.

title: Poverty
question: Have I been attached to material goods?
description: Poverty calls for detachment (CCC 2544).
counsels: Live simply.

title: Reverence for God
question: Have I taken the Lord’s name in vain seriously or blasphemed?
description: Blasphemy is speaking against God with hatred, reproach, or defiance and is gravely sinful (CCC 2148). It violates reverence owed to God’s name.
counsels: Acknowledge the seriousness of your words.
Repair by acts of reverence (prayer, adoration).
prevention: Avoid environments where blasphemy is normalized

title: Truth
question: Have I committed serious lying or deception that harmed others?
description: Lying is speaking falsehood with intent to deceive (CCC 2482). Grave lies cause serious harm and are mortal.
counsels: Repair the harm done.
Confess clearly.

title: Justice
question: Have I stolen something of significant value?
description: Theft violates justice and requires restitution (CCC 2408–2412).
counsels: Return what you took.
Make restitution.

title: Charity
question: Have I hated someone or wished serious harm upon them?
description: Hatred of neighbor is contrary to charity and can be gravely sinful (CCC 2303).
counsels: Pray for the person.
Choose forgiveness.

title: Charity
question: Have I refused to forgive someone who seriously hurt me?
description: Refusal to forgive blocks God’s mercy (CCC 2840).
counsels: Forgiveness is a choice.
Release resentment.

title: Temperance
question: Have I abused alcohol to the point of drunkenness?
description: Drunkenness impairs reason and dignity (CCC 2290).
counsels: Seek accountability.
Reduce or eliminate use.

title: Temperance
question: Have I used drugs or substances that harm my body or mind?
description: Drug use is a grave offense against health and life (CCC 2291).
counsels: Seek help immediately.
Confess sincerely.

title: Chastity
question: Have I deliberately entertained lustful thoughts?
description: Lust is disordered desire for sexual pleasure (CCC 2351).
counsels: Reject thoughts immediately.
Redirect attention.

title: Chastity
question: Have I placed myself in near occasions of sexual sin?
description: Knowingly entering temptation risks grave sin (CCC 2119).
counsels: Identify triggers.
Remove them.

title: Love of Neighbor
question: Have I seriously damaged someone’s reputation (calumny or detraction)?
description: Revealing faults unjustly harms others (CCC 2477).
counsels: Repair the damage.
Speak truth with charity.

title: Justice
question: Have I cheated in school, work, or business?
description: Fraud and cheating violate justice (CCC 2409).
counsels: Make restitution.
Be honest going forward.

title: Faith
question: Have I publicly denied my faith out of fear or shame?
description: Failing to witness to Christ can be grave (CCC 1816).
counsels: Repent and stand firm.
Strengthen courage.

title: Humility
question: Have I acted out of pride, placing myself above God or others?
description: Pride is the root of sin (CCC 1866).
counsels: Practice humility.
Serve others.

title: Sloth
question: Have I neglected my responsibilities out of laziness?
description: Sloth is spiritual and physical laziness (CCC 2733).
counsels: Build discipline.
Start small.

title: Charity
question: Have I ignored the needs of the poor or suffering?
description: Neglecting charity is sinful (CCC 2447).
counsels: Be generous.
Serve actively.

title: Justice
question: Have I wasted money irresponsibly or been overly materialistic?
description: Attachment to wealth leads away from God (CCC 2424).
counsels: Simplify life.
Prioritize God.

title: Temperance
question: Have I been gluttonous or lacked self-control?
description: Gluttony is disordered consumption (CCC 1866).
counsels: Practice moderation.

title: Love of Neighbor
question: Have I judged others rashly without sufficient evidence?
description: Rash judgment violates charity (CCC 2478).
counsels: Assume good intentions.

title: Faith
question: Have I placed relationships, career, or success above God?
description: Idolatry places created things above God (CCC 2113).
counsels: Reorder priorities.

title: Prayer Life
question: Have I ignored God’s call in my life or failed to discern my vocation?
description: Each person has a calling from God (CCC 2232).
counsels: Pray for clarity.
Seek guidance.

title: Fidelity
question: Have I engaged in emotional infidelity or an inappropriate attachment to someone other than my spouse?
description: Emotional affairs violate the exclusivity of marital love and can lead to adultery (CCC 2380–2381).
counsels: Cut off all inappropriate contact immediately.
Be transparent with your spouse.
Seek marriage counseling or spiritual direction.
prevention: Set clear boundaries with members of the opposite sex
Share your struggles with your spouse regularly
Avoid private messaging with former romantic interests

title: Openness to Life
question: Have I used artificial contraception to prevent pregnancy?
description: Contraception separates the unitive and procreative meanings of the marital act and is gravely disordered (CCC 2370).
counsels: Learn and practice Natural Family Planning (NFP).
Discuss openness to life with your spouse.
Confess and seek guidance from a faithful priest.

title: Charity
question: Have I neglected my spouse emotionally, spiritually, or physically?
description: Spouses are called to mutual self-giving; neglect undermines the marriage covenant (CCC 1643).
counsels: Schedule regular time together without distractions.
Ask your spouse how they feel loved.
Prioritize your marriage over work and hobbies.

title: Leadership
question: Have I failed to lead my family in faith, prayer, or moral formation?
description: Parents are the primary educators of their children and must guide them toward God (CCC 2223–2226).
counsels: Start family prayer, even if briefly.
Teach your children about the faith by example.
Take initiative rather than waiting for your spouse.
prevention: Sunday Mass as a non-negotiable family habit
Read a Bible story together at meals

title: Charity
question: Have I verbally or emotionally abused my spouse or children?
description: Verbal and emotional abuse violates the dignity of the person and destroys trust in the family (CCC 2302–2303).
counsels: Apologize sincerely and commit to change.
Seek professional counseling or anger management.
Ask your family for forgiveness.

title: Prayer Life
question: Have I failed to pray with or for my spouse and family?
description: The domestic church is strengthened by shared prayer; neglecting it weakens the family's spiritual life (CCC 2685).
counsels: Begin with one shared prayer a day (grace before meals, bedtime).
Invite your spouse to pray together, even briefly.
Pray for your spouse's intentions daily.
prevention: Set a specific time for family prayer
Attend Mass together as a family

title: Justice
question: Have I been financially irresponsible, putting my family at risk through reckless spending, gambling, or debt?
description: Spouses have a duty of justice to provide for their family's material needs responsibly (CCC 2409).
counsels: Create a family budget together.
Confess any hidden debts or spending to your spouse.
Seek financial counseling if needed.
prevention: Monthly budget review with your spouse

title: Mercy
question: Have I refused to forgive my spouse or held grudges in our marriage?
description: Forgiveness is essential to married love; refusing it hardens the heart and damages the bond (CCC 2843).
counsels: Choose forgiveness as an act of the will, even when feelings resist.
Bring unresolved hurts to Confession and prayer.
Seek couples counseling for deep wounds.
prevention: Never go to bed angry — resolve conflicts daily
Pray the Our Father together, reflecting on 'forgive us as we forgive'
Practice small acts of kindness after disagreements

title: Chastity
question: Have I refused the marital debt or used my spouse for selfish sexual gratification?
description: Spouses owe mutual self-giving; selfishness or coercion in the marital act violates its meaning (CCC 2362–2363).
counsels: Discuss intimacy honestly and charitably with your spouse.
Put your spouse's needs before your own.
Seek guidance from a faithful priest if needed.
prevention: Read Theology of the Body together

title: Openness to Life
question: Have I pressured my spouse toward sterilization or abortion?
description: Direct sterilization and abortion are gravely sinful; they reject God's gift of life (CCC 2271, 2399).
counsels: Repent and confess immediately.
If sterilization occurred, seek pastoral guidance on reversal or acceptance.
Support your spouse through healing.
prevention: Learn Church teaching on life issues together

title: Temperance
question: Have I prioritized work, hobbies, or screens over quality time with my family?
description: Neglecting family for distractions fails in the duty of love owed to spouse and children (CCC 2211).
counsels: Set firm boundaries around work hours.
Put devices away during family time.
Be fully present to your spouse and children.
prevention: Limit social media to set times

title: Truth
question: Have I lied to my spouse about serious matters or lived a double life?
description: Lying destroys trust, the foundation of marriage; serious deception is gravely sinful (CCC 2482–2484).
counsels: Confess the truth to your spouse with humility.
Accept the consequences of your dishonesty.
Rebuild trust through consistent transparency.
prevention: Practice radical honesty, even about small things
Share passwords and financial information openly
Seek accountability from a trusted friend or mentor

title: Charity
question: Have I spoken badly about my spouse to others or publicly humiliated them?
description: Detraction against one's spouse wounds their reputation and violates marital charity (CCC 2477).
counsels: Apologize to your spouse and to those you spoke to.
Speak positively about your spouse in public.
Bring complaints to your spouse directly, not to others.
prevention: Follow the rule: praise in public, correct in private
Vent only to a spiritual director or counselor
Pray for your spouse when frustrated

title: Faith
question: Have I failed to raise my children in the Catholic faith or neglected their sacramental preparation?
description: Parents promise at Baptism to raise children in the faith; neglecting this duty is a serious failure (CCC 2225–2226).
counsels: Enroll children in religious education.
Teach them basic prayers and the meaning of the sacraments.
Be an example of faith they can follow.
prevention: Discuss faith naturally in everyday life

title: Patience
question: Have I been impatient, harsh, or dismissive toward my children?
description: Parents must guide children with patience and love, not provoke them to anger (CCC 2223; Eph 6:4).
counsels: Apologize to your children when you lose your temper.
Discipline with calm and reason, not rage.
Spend one-on-one time with each child.

title: Humility
question: Have I refused to seek help for my marriage when it was struggling?
description: Pride can prevent spouses from seeking the help they need, allowing wounds to deepen (CCC 1608).
counsels: Reach out to a priest, deacon, or Catholic marriage counselor.
Attend a marriage enrichment program or retreat.
Be honest about the state of your marriage.
prevention: Annual marriage retreat or enrichment weekend
Maintain friendships with other faithful married couples

title: Chastity
question: Have I viewed pornography or engaged in lustful fantasies about someone other than my spouse?
description: Pornography and lustful fantasies violate marital fidelity and the dignity of the human person (CCC 2354, 2528).
counsels: Confess immediately and install content filters.
Tell your spouse if appropriate and seek accountability.
Join a recovery group or program.
prevention: Install accountability software on all devices
Avoid being alone with screens late at night
Pray the Rosary daily for purity

title: Obedience
question: Have I persistently refused to obey my legitimate superiors in matters of community life?
description: Religious obedience is a participation in Christ's own obedience and a vow freely made to God (CCC 915, 2103).
counsels: Examine the root cause of your resistance — pride, fear, or legitimate concern.
Speak openly with your superior about your struggles.
Recommit your will to God through your vow.
prevention: Daily renewal of vows in morning prayer
Meditate on Christ's obedience in Gethsemane

title: Chastity
question: Have I entertained romantic or sexual fantasies, or formed an exclusive emotional attachment?
description: Consecrated chastity requires undivided love for God; exclusive attachments undermine this gift (CCC 2349, 916).
counsels: Bring the attachment to your spiritual director immediately.
Set firm boundaries with the person involved.
Redirect your affection toward Christ through prayer.
prevention: Custody of the eyes and imagination
Avoid private one-on-one meetings that create intimacy
Nurture friendships within healthy community settings

title: Poverty
question: Have I secretly kept money, possessions, or privileges for myself against my community's rule?
description: The vow of poverty calls for detachment from material goods and sharing all things in common (CCC 2544–2545).
counsels: Surrender hidden items or funds to your superior.
Confess attachments honestly.
Embrace simplicity as a path to freedom.
prevention: Meditate on Christ's poverty in the manger and on the cross

title: Prayer Life
question: Have I habitually skipped the Liturgy of the Hours, community prayer, or personal meditation?
description: Prayer is the foundation of consecrated life; neglecting it leads to spiritual dryness and loss of vocation (CCC 2691, 2744).
counsels: Return to your prayer schedule starting today.
Bring your dryness to spiritual direction.
Start small if overwhelmed — even five minutes of silent presence.
prevention: Fixed prayer schedule with community accountability
Treat prayer times as sacred appointments
Vary prayer forms to combat routine (lectio, adoration, Rosary)

title: Truth
question: Have I caused scandal by living contrary to the vows I profess publicly?
description: Scandal leads others to sin and is especially grave when caused by those who teach or model the faith (CCC 2284–2285).
counsels: Acknowledge the harm your witness has caused.
Confess and make amends where possible.
Renew your commitment to authentic witness.
prevention: Live as if your actions are always observed — because they are by God
Regular examen focused on public witness
Ask a trusted peer for honest feedback

title: Humility
question: Have I acted out of pride in my ministry, seeking recognition or control?
description: Pride in ministry turns service into self-promotion and is contrary to the spirit of Christ the servant (CCC 1831, 2540).
counsels: Offer your ministry back to God each morning.
Accept hidden tasks without complaint.
Celebrate others' gifts without envy.
prevention: Begin each ministry task with a prayer of surrender
Seek the lowest place at gatherings
Fast from talking about your accomplishments

title: Charity
question: Have I been uncharitable, critical, or cold toward members of my religious community?
description: Community life demands patient charity; persistent unkindness wounds the Body of Christ (CCC 1822, 2447).
counsels: Apologize to anyone you've hurt.
Practice small acts of kindness toward the person you find most difficult.
Examine whether your criticism reflects projections of your own faults.
prevention: Daily prayer for each community member by name
Speak about others only what you would say to their face
Practice the 'little way' of small, hidden sacrifices

title: Justice
question: Have I misused authority over those entrusted to my care?
description: Authority in religious life must serve, not dominate; misuse is a grave violation of justice (CCC 2235–2236).
counsels: Examine how you exercise power — is it for others or for yourself?
Seek forgiveness from those you've mistreated.
Submit to periodic reviews of your leadership.
prevention: Model servant leadership after Christ washing feet
Maintain open channels for feedback from those you lead
Regular reading on the spirituality of leadership

title: Love of God
question: Have I celebrated the Eucharist or sacraments routinely, without reverence or interior devotion?
description: The Eucharist demands interior participation; habitual irreverence diminishes its transformative power (CCC 1387–1388).
counsels: Prepare for Mass with silent prayer beforehand.
Renew your awareness of Christ's Real Presence.
Make a thanksgiving after receiving Communion.
prevention: Monthly day of recollection focused on the Eucharist

title: Obedience
question: Have I murmured against or undermined my superior's decisions behind their back?
description: Murmuring and gossip against authority erode community trust and violate the spirit of obedience (CCC 2477, 2103).
counsels: Bring your disagreements directly to your superior.
Stop participating in circles of complaint.
Pray for your superiors daily.
prevention: Practice the discipline of direct communication
Offer a Hail Mary when tempted to complain

title: Poverty
question: Have I lived extravagantly or sought unnecessary comforts contrary to my vow?
description: Religious poverty witnesses to detachment from worldly goods; indulgence contradicts this witness (CCC 2544–2546).
counsels: Simplify your living space and habits.
Return unnecessary items to the community.
Embrace discomfort as a share in Christ's poverty.
prevention: Periodic simplicity audit of your room and possessions
Ask before buying: 'Do I need this, or want this?'
Volunteer for the less comfortable assignments

title: Truth
question: Have I been hypocritical — presenting a holy image externally while living differently in private?
description: Hypocrisy is a form of lying that gravely harms one's own soul and others' trust in the faith (CCC 2468, 2505).
counsels: Be honest in spiritual direction about your interior life.
Let your private life match your public witness.
Confess not just actions but patterns of duplicity.
prevention: Cultivate one trusted confidant who sees the real you
Pray 'Lord, let me be the same person in public and in private'

title: Temperance
question: Have I been intemperate with food, drink, or digital media in a way that harms my spiritual life?
description: Temperance moderates desires and is essential for religious witness; excess dulls the spiritual senses (CCC 1809, 2290).
counsels: Fast or abstain as a concrete act of self-denial.
Set strict limits on media and entertainment consumption.
Replace indulgent habits with prayer or service.
prevention: Follow your community's fasting practices faithfully
Weekly fasting beyond the minimum required

title: Charity
question: Have I gossiped about or detracted from the reputation of fellow religious?
description: Detraction unjustly harms another's reputation and wounds community life (CCC 2477, 2479).
counsels: Apologize to anyone whose reputation you damaged.
Repair the harm by speaking well of them to the same audience.
Commit to speaking only what is true, necessary, and kind.
prevention: Apply the 'three gates' test: Is it true? Is it necessary? Is it kind?
Leave conversations that turn to gossip
Pray for those you are tempted to criticize

title: Zeal
question: Have I become complacent in my vocation, going through the motions without love?
description: Lukewarmness in consecrated life is spiritual acedia — a weariness that refuses God's joy (CCC 2094, 2733).
counsels: Renew your vows intentionally and prayerfully.
Ask God to rekindle your first love.
Seek a new apostolate or ministry to revive your zeal.
prevention: Annual retreat focused on your vocation story
Serve in a new capacity periodically
Read the lives of saints who shared your charism

title: Justice
question: Have I used community resources selfishly or wasted what belongs to the community?
description: Community goods are held in trust; wasting them violates justice and the common good (CCC 2404–2405).
counsels: Return or replace what you misused.
Discuss resource use openly in community meetings.
Practice gratitude for what is provided.
prevention: Ask permission before using shared resources for personal needs

title: Faith
question: Have I allowed intellectual pride to distance me from simple, childlike faith?
description: Intellectual pride can replace trust in God with trust in one's own understanding (CCC 2088, 1831).
counsels: Approach Scripture and prayer with a beginner's heart.
Submit your intellect to the faith of the Church.
Seek wisdom, not just knowledge.
prevention: Pray the Litany of Humility regularly
Study under obedience, not solely personal interest
Spend time with simple, faith-filled people
